source_pr.title: planner, statistics: rewrite dynamic partition analyze for legacy stats version
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #63579

Problem Summary:

Dynamic partition analyze could rebuild global stats with the session-selected analyze version while some persisted table or partition stats were still on legacy version 1.

### What changed and how does it work?

When dynamic `ANALYZE TABLE ... PARTITION ...` sees a stats-version mismatch, it now expands to all partitions before the global-stats merge. This PR also adds manual-analyze and auto-analyze regressions for the rewrite path and removes the related stale `FIXME`s.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Detects and rewrites legacy statistics versions correctly for partitioned tables and shows a warning when a rewrite occurs.

* **Refactor**
  * Streamlined analyze-version resolution to consistently validate global and per-partition statistics.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded tests to cover partitioned tables, assert rewrite behavior and warnings, and verify persisted stats versions update.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

symptoms: Analyze/plan-stats tests were flaky around legacy (v1) stats behavior and dynamic partition analyze, causing nondeterministic stats/explain assertions.
failure_signature: Legacy/v1 stats + dynamic partition analyze leading to nondeterministic stats/explain assertions.
fix_pattern: Rewrite dynamic partition analyze flow for legacy stats and add tests that simulate persisted v1 stats, improving determinism of analyze/plan-stats assertions.

evidence: evidence: Subject: [PATCH 5/6] planner: stabilize partial stats explain test

analysis:
PR title: planner, statistics: rewrite dynamic partition analyze for legacy stats version
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/67178
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: Subject: [PATCH 5/6] planner: stabilize partial stats explain test
Changed files (14): pkg/executor/test/analyzetest/BUILD.bazel, pkg/executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go, pkg/planner/core/casetest/planstats/plan_stats_test.go, pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go, pkg/planner/core/tests/analyze/BUILD.bazel, pkg/planner/core/tests/analyze/analyze_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/autoanalyze/autoanalyze.go, pkg/statistics/handle/autoanalyze/exec/exec_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/autoanalyze/priorityqueue/analysis_job_factory.go, pkg/statistics/handle/globalstats/global_stats.go ...
Diff highlights (excerpt):
- "select table_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id in (?,?,?) group by table_id, stats_ver order by table_id",
- "select hist_id, stats_ver from mysql.stats_histograms where table_id = %d and is_index = 0 order by hist_id",
- // the stats version eventually persisted for the same global stats.
Full patch analyzed; see patch_url.
